message:
fix(dirent,stdlib,rpc): scandir/realpath/user2netname use libc::malloc (bd-zgifl)

Continuing the bd-zgifl cross-allocator-free sweep with five more
sites across three modules. All POSIX functions whose contract
explicitly says "the caller frees the result with free()":

- dirent_abi::scandir entry copy   (line 524)
- dirent_abi::scandir empty array  (line 556)
- dirent_abi::scandir array        (line 567)
- stdlib_abi::realpath              (line 4026, resolved_path == NULL path)
- rpc_abi::user2netname             (line 2995, sibling of host2netname)

Each was allocating via crate::malloc_abi::raw_alloc which routes
through native_libc_malloc; under NATIVE_MALLOC_REENTRY contention,
that falls back to a static BUMP_HEAP arena returning pointers
glibc's free() cannot validate ("free(): invalid size" abort).

Switching all five to libc::malloc keeps the alloc/free pair
consistent: under LD_PRELOAD libc::malloc IS our interposed symbol,
under non-preload it's glibc's; in both cases the caller's
libc::free pairs correctly. Also updated the in-module cleanup
paths (dirent_abi error rollbacks) from raw_free → libc::free to
stay symmetric with the new malloc.

## Sites NOT touched (deliberately)

inet_abi::if_nameindex (lines 382, 393): pairs with our own
if_freenameindex which uses raw_free internally — alloc/free
contract is fully internal, no cross-allocator risk.

rpc_abi clntudp_create internal buffers (lines 666, 667): freed
by clnt_destroy via internal cleanup, never returned to caller.

rpc_abi XDR codec sites (lines 1091, 1151, 1228, 1293): XDR's
allocation contract is "use xdr_free", not free(). Not safe to
flip without auditing every xdr_free call site for matching
allocator. Leaving for a dedicated XDR audit.

## Validation

cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test dirent_abi_test  -> 31 passed
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test stdlib_abi_test  -> 202 passed
cargo test -p frankenlibc-abi --test rpc_abi_test     -> 35 passed

bd-zgifl progress: 8 of ~30 sites now fixed (host2netname, strdup,
strndup, wcsdup, scandir x3, realpath, user2netname). Remaining
~22 sites in unistd_abi (13), wchar_abi (2 internal-use), inet_abi
(2 internal-use), string_abi (1), and the deferred XDR cluster.

Agent: cc-libc
Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
